authoritative_sources.title: DoDI 5000.02, Operation of the Defense Acquisition System
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary defense acquisition and program governance directive defining lifecycle authority boundaries, oversight moments, and program transition obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program control intake and lifecycle boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS Part 215
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative defense acquisition workflow and documentation expectations for contracting conditions and control records.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.204-7012, Safeguarding Covered Defense Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Directly governs evidence handling, safeguarding, and access controls for controlled defense information used in this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-171, Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines evidence control and security-control expectations used for readiness checks and nonpublic artifact handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control evidence sufficiency and security gating

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61r2, Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides incident handling expectations and handoff behavior necessary for recurring incident and escalation scenarios.

authoritative_sources.title: MIL-STD-882E, System Safety
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Reference for safety taxonomy and hazard-aware handling where mission-readiness and control gates intersect with safety obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety escalation and risk priority handling
used_for: refusal conditions for non-authorized safety closure

authoritative_sources.title: NAICS Sector Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Industry terminology and record normalization source
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public sector-classification baseline for terminology and artifact shape that distinguishes this lane from generic program-control behavior.

workflow_stages.name: Program control intake and authority capture
workflow_stages.description: Validate program context, mission status, owner matrix, and legal/safety/compliance context before any recommendation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and control gate assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect and validate nonconformance, defect, incident, and release evidence and map required controls to requested output stage.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and dependency enforcement
workflow_stages.description: Apply hold/release, authority, and system-of-record dependency boundaries with explicit escalation when unresolved.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Safety, incident, and conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route incidents, repeat defects, and source-policy conflicts to qualified owners without autonomous resolution.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Tenant adaptation and closure
workflow_stages.description: Record tenant-specific assumptions and adaptions before closing controls or issuing control recommendations.
source_classes: Industry terminology and record normalization source
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Program control plan and phase logs
Readiness declarations and mission impact notes
Release/hold records and waiver exception packets

allowed: Assemble defensible control summaries, hold-release decisions, and defect/incidence handoff outputs.
Route to proper adjacent owners when authority, legal, finance, or formal QA context is outside scope.
Escalate unresolved evidence gaps, conflicts, and tenant-policy mismatches.
escalate: Tenant mission policy or authority matrix is missing and materially changes outcome.
Open incidents or recurring defects need licensed safety/QA owner input.
System dependency failures block evidence retrieval.
Public source and tenant policy conflict cannot be resolved.
refuse: Requests for legal, financial, licensing, or final safety authority
Requests to close defects or incidents without sufficient evidence

domain_failure_modes: Treating generic program-control steps as sufficient and ignoring mission-specific readiness obligations.
Attempting release when nonconformance, CAPA, or incident evidence is incomplete or stale.
Resolving safety or legal conflicts without approved authority.
Closing repeat defects without measurable recurrence analysis and owner-approved mitigation.
Ignoring source-of-truth outages and routing dependencies before owner resolution.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
A required control is missing evidence
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves public baseline
Repeat defect or recurring incident handling

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific mission criticality categories and override thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different programs use different definitions for mission-essential and readiness blocking criteria.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant mission dictionary and authority matrix as required context before high-confidence progression.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether recurring defects must move to work-stopping control versus corrective-action loop
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Program policy may impose mandatory stop-work requirements not visible in public sources.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate recurrence cases to formal program owner and retain block posture until tenant policy confirms next step.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local interpretation of formal safety-owner authority
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Whether safety authority lies with contract safety office, program safety engineer, or mission systems QA varies by tenant.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Do not infer; request authority owner and escalate when missing.

notes: Public baseline includes defense acquisition directives and control standards; tenant policy is required for final authority, mission-critical definitions, and escalation ownership.
Source set covers workflow, terminology, evidence control, hold/release, incident escalation, and conflict-handling dimensions.
No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain after converting all open points into required assumptions or escalation conditions.
